title: 10 best herbal essential oils for loss of appetite
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for loss of appetite are natural, plant-based substances that can help stimulate hunger and improve digestion. They work by affecting the brain's satiety centers, increasing feelings of fullness and reducing cravings. Examples of herbal essential oils that promote appetite include ginger, peppermint, and fennel. These oils have been shown to improve nutritional intake in individuals with eating disorders, Alzheimer's disease, and cancer patients undergoing chemotherapy, leading to better overall health and well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with loss of appetite because they stimulate digestive enzymes, which can increase hunger and reduce nausea. The warming properties of ginger also help to alleviate stomach discomfort, making it easier for individuals to eat and digest food properly. Additionally, the aroma of ginger has been shown to have a stimulating effect on the appetite centers in the brain, promoting feelings of hunger and increasing the desire to eat.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Ginger oil helps stimulate digestive enzymes, which can aid in breaking down food and increasing appetite. This property can be particularly helpful for individuals who experience nausea or indigestion related to decreased appetite.

Anti-Inflammatory: Ginger oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which may contribute to loss of appetite due to discomfort or pain. By reducing inflammation, ginger oil can promote a more comfortable digestive environment that encourages eating.

Vasodilator: Ginger oil's vasodilatory effects can help increase blood flow to the digestive system, promoting healthy digestion and nutrient absorption. This increased blood flow may also help stimulate appetite by delivering essential nutrients to the body.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help stimulate appetite and improve digestion.
Roots: Ginger roots contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have been shown to increase appetite and reduce nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh ginger rhizomes of 2-3 inches long and wash them thoroughly.
Dry the ginger slices in a single layer on paper towels for 7 hours.
Steam distill the dried ginger at 100°c for 30 minutes using 500ml water.
Collect the essential oil obtained from the distillation process and store it in dark glass bottles.
Mix 10 drops of the essential oil with 1 tablespoon of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with loss of appetite because its invigorating aroma stimulates digestion and increases salivary secretions, which can help to rekindle a healthy appetite. Additionally, peppermint's calming properties can soothe digestive discomfort, such as bloating or nausea, that may be contributing to a lack of interest in food. By reducing feelings of indigestion and discomfort, peppermint essential oil can help to restore a sense of well-being and promote a renewed desire for meals.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Peppermint essential oil helps stimulate digestion and relieve indigestion, which can be a major contributor to loss of appetite. Its menthol content relaxes the digestive tract, reducing spasms and inflammation that can cause discomfort and nausea.

Carminative: Peppermint essential oil's carminative properties help eliminate gas and bloating from the digestive system, making it easier for people with appetite issues to feel more comfortable eating. This reduction in discomfort can encourage a return of appetite.

Anxiolytic: The calming effects of peppermint essential oil's menthol content have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate anxiety and stress that often accompany loss of appetite. By reducing anxiety, peppermint essential oil may increase the willingness to eat.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots are known for their calming properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues and improve appetite in individuals with loss of appetite.
Leaves: The leaves of the peppermint plant contain menthol, a natural compound that stimulates digestion and can increase appetite in people who have lost their taste for food.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves by cutting them at the base when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator or oven on low heat for 24 hours.
Crush the dried peppermint leaves into smaller pieces using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to release essential oils.
Steam distill the crushed peppermint leaves using a steam distillation apparatus at 100-110 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Collect and filter the extracted essential oil from the distillation apparatus through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with loss of appetite because its potent digestive properties stimulate the appetite and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and nausea. The oil's natural anise-like aroma also helps to increase saliva production, which can help to break down food more efficiently, making it easier to digest and absorb nutrients. Additionally, fennel essential oils may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, further improving overall gut health and promoting a healthy appetite.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Fennel essential oil stimulates digestion and improves gut motility, which can help increase appetite by relieving digestive discomfort and bloating.

Anti-Inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of fennel essential oil may help reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion and loss of appetite.
Anxiolytic: Fennel essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that may be contributing to loss of appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in volatile oils and flavonoids, making them effective in stimulating appetite and digestion.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a high concentration of anethole, a compound that has been shown to increase appetite and alleviate digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems in the early morning.
Dry the harvested plant material using a clean drying rack for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried plant material with 5 liters of distilled water at 100°c for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter and store it in dark glass bottles.
Blend the foeniculum vulgare essential oil with jojoba oil in a ratio of 10% to 90%.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange essential oils helps with loss of appetite because it stimulates the digestive system, increasing gut contractions and peristalsis. This natural stimulation encourages the breakdown and absorption of nutrients, helping to alleviate feelings of fullness and bloating. Additionally, the citrus scent of bitter orange oil has a uplifting effect on the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can often lead to loss of appetite. By promoting digestive health and relaxation, bitter orange essential oils may help restore appetite and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Stomachic and Digestive Properties: Bitter orange essential oil helps stimulate digestion, relieve indigestion and nausea, and increase appetite by promoting healthy gut function, which can alleviate feelings of fullness or discomfort associated with a lack of appetite.

Adaptogenic and Stress-Relieving Properties: The oil's adaptogenic properties help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of loss of appetite. By calming the nervous system, bitter orange essential oil can increase appetite by promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being.

Stimulant and Uterotonic Properties: Bitter orange essential oil has stimulant properties that can increase circulation and improve digestion, helping to boost energy levels and stimulate the appetite. Additionally, its uterotonic properties may help relieve menstrual cramps and other hormonal imbalances that can cause loss of appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the bitter orange plant contain a high concentration of alkaloids, which can help stimulate appetite and improve digestion.
Seeds: Bitter orange seeds have been traditionally used to increase appetite and relieve digestive issues, making them a valuable part for essential oil production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality citrus x aurantium leaves at dawn in late summer for optimal citrusy flavor profile.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator set to 60°c for 24 hours or air-drying them for 7 days.
Combine 200g of dried citrus x aurantium leaves with 400ml of high-proof vodka in a glass jar for infusion.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the jar daily, to extract essential oils and flavors.
Filter the infused liquid using a coffee filter or cheesecloth and store the resulting citrus x aurantium essential oil in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with loss of appetite because they have a soothing and calming effect on the digestive system, reducing feelings of discomfort and bloating that can often accompany lack of appetite. The oil's citrusy aroma also stimulates the appetite centers in the brain, encouraging hunger and increasing desire for food. Additionally, lemon balm has anti-inflammatory properties that may help to ease digestive issues that can lead to loss of appetite.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Lemon balm essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate feelings of anxiety and stress that may contribute to loss of appetite.

Digestive stimulant properties: The essential oil contains compounds that can stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and nausea, making it easier for people with loss of appetite to consume food.

Stomach soother properties: Lemon balm essential oil has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects that can help soothe an irritable stomach, reducing discomfort and pain that may be causing decreased appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots contain a high concentration of essential oils that have been shown to stimulate appetite and improve digestion, making them an effective choice for treating loss of appetite.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant are rich in camphor and citral, which have natural appetizing properties that can help increase hunger and improve food cravings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at peak growth stage for maximum oil yield.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or machine drying methods for two days.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a still using 100 grams per liter water ratio at 100°c temperature for one hour.
Filter the essential oil from the distilled mixture using paper or cloth filtration and store it in dark glass bottles.
Dilute the essential oil with carrier oils such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a 2% concentration for topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric essential oils helps with loss of appetite because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe digestive issues, such as bloating and discomfort. This relief from digestive discomfort can stimulate the appetite, encouraging individuals to eat more regularly. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant compounds may also help to improve nutrient absorption, leading to a greater sense of satisfaction and reduced hunger pangs. As a result, incorporating turmeric essential oils into one's daily routine can potentially aid in addressing loss of appetite and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Curcuminoids have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce inflammation in the body that can lead to a decrease in appetite.
Antioxidant properties: Curcuminoids have strong antioxidant properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage that can contribute to loss of appetite.
Digestive benefits: Curcuminoids have been shown to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), which can also lead to a decrease in appetite.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Containing curcuminoids, a potent anti-inflammatory compound, which can stimulate appetite by reducing inflammation and improving digestion.
Roots: Rich in vitamins and minerals, roots help to increase nutrient absorption and promote healthy gut function, leading to an improved appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric roots from organic farms or your own garden weighing about 100 grams.
Dry the harvested turmeric roots for at least 7 days in a warm and dry place away from direct sunlight.
Grind the dried turmeric roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered turmeric with carrier oil like coconut oil in a ratio of 1:2 and simmer it at 150°f for 6 hours in a double boiler setup.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the essential oils from the solids and store it in dark glass bottles for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with loss of appetite because it possesses a unique composition that stimulates the digestive system, increasing hunger and improving nutrient absorption. The oil's bioactive compounds also exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate digestive discomfort and bloating often associated with loss of appetite. Additionally, the calming and soothing effects of maidenhair tree essential oils may reduce stress and anxiety-related eating disorders, promoting a healthy appetite and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties may help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to a lack of appetite.
Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels may improve blood flow to the brain, potentially increasing feelings of hunger and improving appetite.
Serotonin regulation: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to modulate serotonin levels in the body, which can help regulate appetite and food intake.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain bioactive compounds that help stimulate digestion and increase appetite, making them an effective part in creating medicinal essential oils for loss of appetite.

Barks: The bark of the maidenhair tree contains tannins and other polyphenolic compounds that have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and increase appetite, making it a valuable component in creating essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried ginkgo biloba leaves in the morning when they are still turgid with dew for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60 degrees celsius for 6 hours to prevent mold growth.
Combine one pound of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with one pint of carrier oil like sweet almond or jojoba in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure uniform extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids, then store the essential oil in an amber glass bottle away from light.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with loss of appetite because of its uplifting and calming properties, which can stimulate digestion and improve overall gut health. The citrusy scent of lemongrass oil has been shown to increase salivation and enhance the appetite by activating the brain's reward centers, making food more appealing. Additionally, lemongrass oil's antispasmodic properties help relieve digestive discomforts, such as bloating and cramps, which can be a common cause of loss of appetite.
remedy_properties: Digestive stimulant: Lemongrass essential oil stimulates digestion, which can help increase appetite by improving gut function and reducing digestive discomfort. This property helps to relieve symptoms such as indigestion, bloating, and constipation that may be contributing to a lack of appetite.

Anti-inflammatory: Lemongrass essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is often associated with loss of appetite. By reducing inflammation, lemongrass oil can help alleviate symptoms such as nausea, abdominal pain, and discomfort that may be making it difficult to eat.

Uplifting and calming: Lemongrass essential oil has a calming and uplifting effect on the mind and body, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may be contributing to a loss of appetite. By promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of overwhelm, lemongrass oil can help increase hunger and improve overall mood.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemongrass roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in stimulating appetite and alleviating digestive issues associated with loss of appetite.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is the thick, underground stem of lemongrass, rich in essential oils that help to stimulate digestion, improve appetite, and alleviate nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves from your garden or local market in the early morning for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on paper towels at room temperature for 3-5 days to reduce moisture content.
Crush 250g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to release essential oils.
Steam distill the crushed cymbopogon citratus mixture with 500ml of distilled water at 100°c for 2 hours to extract the essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil from the distillation process through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and store it in an airtight bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with loss of appetite because it has a calming effect on the stomach, reducing inflammation and soothing digestive issues that can lead to a decrease in appetite. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help to regulate digestion, allowing the body to properly absorb nutrients and reduce symptoms such as nausea and bloating. Additionally, sage's aromatic compounds have been shown to stimulate the appetite center of the brain, increasing hunger and desire for food.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal sage essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate digestive discomfort and inflammation in the stomach, which can contribute to loss of appetite.
Digestive stimulant properties: The essential oil has been shown to stimulate digestion and improve gut motility, which can help increase appetite and reduce symptoms of indigestion and nausea.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal sage essential oil's antioxidants may help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to loss of appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant contain a high concentration of camphor and borneol, which are known to stimulate appetite and improve digestion.
Seeds: Sage seeds contain a compound called thujone, which has been shown to have anorexic properties and can help reduce food intake.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves from your garden or a local supplier in the early morning.
Rinse the harvested leaves gently with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steam distill 200g of the rinsed leaves for 3 hours using a traditional steam distillation apparatus.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities.
Transfer the filtered essential oil into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with loss of appetite because it has a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help to stimulate appetite in individuals who are experiencing a lack of interest in food. The oil's ability to soothe and relax the digestive tract can also help to alleviate symptoms such as nausea and bloating, making it easier for the individual to eat and digest their food properly.
remedy_properties: Digestive stimulant: Thyme essential oil helps stimulate digestion, which can aid in increasing appetite by regulating digestive enzymes and improving nutrient absorption. This property helps to combat a lack of appetite caused by digestive issues.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme essential oil has antioxidant properties that help protect the body from free radicals, which can contribute to a decrease in appetite and overall energy levels. By neutralizing free radicals, thyme essential oil may help restore appetite and improve overall health.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate digestive discomfort and inflammation, which is often a contributing factor to loss of appetite. By reducing inflammation, thyme essential oil may help stimulate appetite and improve overall digestive function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have been traditionally used to stimulate digestion and improve appetite due to their rich concentration of volatile compounds.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to enhance digestion, reduce inflammation, and increase appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves from your garden or store-bought thyme in a clean glass jar.
Steam distill the thyme leaves using a clevenger apparatus and collect the resulting oil in a separate container.
Mix the collected oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:3.
Store the blended essential oil in an amber glass bottle, away from direct sunlight and heat sources.
Use the blended essential oil topically on the skin, inhaling its aroma to help stimulate appetite and digestion, as needed.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat loss of appetite with herbal essential oils is by inhaling the aroma of certain herbs through steam inhalation or direct application on the skin. Peppermint oil, for example, has a refreshing scent that can stimulate appetite and improve digestion. Ginger oil also helps alleviate nausea and indigestion, while licorice root oil soothes the stomach and promotes appetite. Apply 2-3 drops of your chosen essential oil to a diffuser or mix with a carrier oil for topical application.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to loss of appetite that are treated with herbal essential oils are digestive issues such as nausea, indigestion, and constipation. Essential oils like Peppermint, Ginger, and Fennel can help alleviate these symptoms by soothing the stomach and improving digestion. Additionally, certain essential oils like Lavender and Bergamot can also help to reduce anxiety and stress, which are often underlying causes of loss of appetite.
